title: Crotalaria sulphizii
taxonomy: Crotalaria sulphizii is classified within the family Fabaceae. This family is commonly known as the legume family, which is one of the largest and most widespread groups of flowering plants. The genus Crotalaria is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Crotalaria sulphizii is closely related to other legumes such as beans, peas, and lentils. Crotalaria sulphizii is further classified into the subfamily Faboideae.

side_effects: Crotalaria sulphizii can be toxic to humans and animals if ingested due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. These toxins can cause liver damage and even lead to liver failure in severe cases. Ingestion of the plant can also result in symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Crotalaria juncea, also known as sunn hemp, is a plant that belongs to the legume family and is a type of shrub or vine native to tropical Asia. The health benefits of Crotalaria juncea include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Crotalaria juncea include flavonoids, alkaloids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Crotalaria juncea is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
